locations.name: Roscommon Main Street
locations.description_template: The wide main street of Roscommon town. Shopfronts with painted signs line both sides — drapers, chandlers, a spirit merchant. A horse and cart stands outside the market house. The {weather} sky hangs over the rooftops. It is {time}.

connections.path_description: the long road south through open countryside to the crossroads
a short walk down the street to the square
the street leading past the infirmary to the coach stop
the road past the abbey hotel toward the parish church

locations.name: Roscommon Market Square
locations.description_template: The market square buzzes on fair days with cattle dealers, egg sellers, and travelling tinkers. The old market house stands at its centre, a solid stone building with arched entries. It is {time}. The weather is {weather}.

connections.path_description: the short walk back up to Main Street
the lane past the courthouse to the abbey hotel

locations.name: Roscommon County Infirmary
locations.description_template: A two-storey stone building where the county surgeon tends to the sick and injured. The smell of carbolic and linen drifts from the open windows. It is {time}. The sky is {weather}.

connections.path_description: a short walk past the yard to the coach stop

locations.name: Roscommon Coach Stop
locations.description_template: The coach stop where the mail coach from Dublin arrives three times a week. Passengers stamp their feet and blow on their hands waiting for the next departure. A schedule board is nailed to the wall. It is {time}. Weather: {weather}.

connections.path_description: the street back to Main Street
a short walk past the yard to the infirmary
the long road south to Athlone, following the coach route

locations.name: Roscommon Castle
locations.description_template: The imposing ruins of Roscommon Castle, a Norman stronghold with massive drum towers at each corner. Cattle graze in the inner ward. Jackdaws wheel above the broken battlements. It is {time}. The {weather} sky frames the ruins.

connections.path_description: a track past the old workhouse walls
the path back through the fields to the parish church

locations.mythological_significance: Built in 1269 by Robert de Ufford, burned and rebuilt many times. The locals say a headless horseman rides from the castle on stormy nights.

locations.name: Abbey Hotel
locations.description_template: A respectable coaching inn near the square. The common room has a good fire and the food is plain but wholesome. Travellers and cattle dealers mingle at the bar. It is {time}. Outside: {weather}.

connections.path_description: the lane back to the market square
the road past the church

locations.name: Roscommon Workhouse
locations.description_template: The bleak stone workhouse stands behind high walls. Even in 1820, years before the Famine, it casts a long shadow over the town. The porter's lodge guards the entrance. It is {time}. The sky is {weather}.

connections.path_description: the track past the castle ruins
a lane leading to the subscription library

locations.name: Roscommon Parish Church
locations.description_template: The parish church of Roscommon, a handsome stone building with a square tower. The churchyard is well-kept, with yew trees along the path. It is {time}. The sky is {weather}.

connections.path_description: the road back to Main Street
the path through the fields to the castle
the road past the abbey hotel
a lane past the rectory to the GAA grounds

locations.name: Roscommon GAA Grounds
locations.description_template: An open field where Roscommon men practice hurling and football. The pitch is marked out with whitewash. A few rough benches line one side. It is {time}. The {weather} sky stretches above.

connections.path_description: the lane past the rectory back to the church

locations.name: Roscommon Subscription Library
locations.description_template: A modest room above a shopfront, lined with shelves of books. The subscription library serves the gentry and the few literate tradesmen of the town. It is {time}. Light filters through the window under a {weather} sky.

connections.path_description: the lane back toward the workhouse
